overall: 3 values may need closer review based on the provided reference ranges.

flags.explanation: Hemoglobin A1c is marked high against the supplied reference range. Confirm this with your clinician, especially if symptoms or prior trends differ.

food: A1c is 5.7%. Rebuild your most carb-heavy meal: protein and vegetables first, then a smaller high-fiber carb portion.
For A1c, cut back on sweet drinks, juice, sweet coffee, desserts, white bread, white rice, and other refined carbs; start with the one you have most often.
exercise: For A1c, try a 10-20 minute easy walk after the meal that usually has the most carbs, if that is safe for you.
lifestyle: Set a simple weekday rule for sweet drinks or desserts, such as saving them for planned times instead of making them an automatic habit.
sleep: A1c can drift upward when sleep is short or irregular. Aim for a steady bedtime/wake time and at least 7 hours when possible.
askDoctor: How this result fits with your history, medications, symptoms, and prior reports.

disclaimer: LabLens explains lab data for general education only. It does not diagnose, treat, or replace a clinician.
